name: The Howard State Bank, Howard, Kansas
description_short: Howard State Bank is an FDIC-insured community bank in Howard, Kansas offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services to personal and business customers.
description_long:
Howard State Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank headquartered in Howard, Kansas. The bank has been serving the local area with traditional banking services and has established a digital presence through their website at howardstatebank.com. As an FDIC member institution, customer deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit security up to applicable limits.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of deposit and lending products for both personal and business customers. On the deposit side, they provide checking accounts, savings accounts, and online banking with bill payment capabilities. For borrowers, they offer personal and business loans. The bank also issues personal and business credit cards. Their digital services include online banking, mobile banking through a dedicated app, mobile deposit capabilities, and a debit card control app that allows customers to set transaction alerts and usage restrictions based on location and spending limits.

Howard State Bank distinguishes itself as a community bank with local roots and personalized service. They emphasize accessibility through multiple digital channels while maintaining physical branch locations. Their debit card control app offers sophisticated fraud prevention features including real-time alerts, GPS-based location controls, regional transaction restrictions, and customizable spending limits by merchant category—features that go beyond basic banking offerings.

As a regional community bank, Howard State Bank is best suited for customers seeking personalized service with modern digital conveniences. The bank's smaller size means it may have more limited product offerings and geographic reach compared to larger institutions, and customers should verify specific product details and rates by contacting the bank directly, as detailed information about specific account features, loan terms, and interest rates is not provided on their website.
services: Debit card control app with alerts and usage restrictions
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Mobile deposit capability available for remote check deposits
Advanced debit card control app with real-time transaction alerts and fraud prevention
Location-based card controls using GPS to restrict transactions by geographic region
Customizable spending limits and merchant category restrictions for enhanced security
Free e-statements available to reduce paper usage
Online bill payment service included with online banking
Dedicated mobile banking app for account access on smartphones
cons: Limited information on interest rates, fees, and specific account terms on website
No details provided about minimum deposit requirements or account minimums
Website lacks information about loan products, terms, rates, or eligibility requirements
No transparent pricing structure or fee schedule publicly available
Limited product comparison tools or detailed feature descriptions for deposit accounts
best_for: Local Howard, Kansas residents seeking community banking with FDIC protection
Small business owners needing business checking, savings, and lending in the region
Customers prioritizing mobile and online banking with advanced fraud prevention features
Individuals wanting debit card controls and real-time transaction monitoring
diagnosis: Howard State Bank is best for local Kansas customers and small business owners seeking FDIC-protected banking with modern digital services including mobile deposit and advanced card controls. The main caveat is that detailed product information, rates, and fees are not available on their website, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for specific terms and account details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
